steps.title: Set up the shadow ratio
steps.content:
The trees and their shadows form similar right triangles at the same time of day, so the height-to-shadow ratio is constant:
$$\frac{10}{5}=\frac{x}{2}$$

steps.title: Solve for the other tree's height
steps.content:
Since $\frac{10}{5}=2$, the other tree's height must be twice its shadow length:
$$x=2\cdot2=4$$
That confirms B.
